{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01F8ThGViYjeUTzU7i4C3euF","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/*\n     2→Copyright 2024 New Vector Ltd.\n     3→Copyright 2016-2019 , 2023 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\n     4→\n     5→SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-only OR GPL-3.0-only OR LicenseRef-Element-Commercial\n     6→Please see LICENSE files in the repository root for full details.\n     7→*/\n     8→\n     9→import { PushProcessor } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/pushprocessor\";\n    10→import {\n    11→    NotificationCountType,\n    12→    ConditionKind,\n    13→    PushRuleActionName,\n    14→    PushRuleKind,\n    15→    TweakName,\n    16→    EventStatus,\n    17→} from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/matrix\";\n    18→\n    19→import type { IPushRule, Room, MatrixClient, MatrixEvent } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/matrix\";\n    20→import { NotificationLevel } from \"./stores/notifications/NotificationLevel\";\n    21→import { doesRoomHaveUnreadMessages, doesRoomOrThreadHaveUnreadMessages } from \"./Unread\";\n    22→import { EffectiveMembership, getEffectiveMembership, isKnockDenied } from \"./utils/membership\";\n    23→import SettingsStore from \"./settings/SettingsStore\";\n    24→import { getMarkedUnreadState } from \"./utils/notifications\";\n    25→\n    26→/**\n    27→ * Gets all pending events in a room that have a status of `EventStatus.NOT_SENT`\n    28→ * and belong to the current thread, if specified.\n    29→ * @param room The room to check.\n    30→ * @param threadId The thread to check. If not specified, no thread filtering is performed.\n    31→ * @returns An array of unsent matrix events.\n    32→ */\n    33→export function getUnsentMessages(room: Room, threadId?: string): MatrixEvent[] {\n    34→    if (!room) {\n    35→        return [];\n    36→    }\n    37→    return room.getPendingEvents().filter(function (ev) {\n    38→        if (ev.status !== EventStatus.NOT_SENT) {\n    39→            return false;\n    40→        }\n    41→        if (threadId && threadId !== ev.threadRootId) {\n    42→            return false;\n    43→        }\n    44→        return true;\n    45→    });\n    46→}\n    47→\n    48→export enum RoomNotifState {\n    49→    AllMessagesLoud = \"all_messages_loud\",\n    50→    AllMessages = \"all_messages\",\n    51→    MentionsOnly = \"mentions_only\",\n    52→    Mute = \"mute\",\n    53→}\n    54→\n    55→export function getRoomNotifsState(client: MatrixClient, roomId: string): RoomNotifState | null {\n    56→    if (client.isGuest()) return RoomNotifState.AllMessages;\n    57→\n    58→    // look through the override rules for a rule affecting this room:\n    59→    // if one exists, it will take precedence.\n    60→    const muteRule = findOverrideMuteRule(client, roomId);\n    61→    if (muteRule) {\n    62→        return RoomNotifState.Mute;\n    63→    }\n    64→\n    65→    // for everything else, look at the room rule.\n    66→    let roomRule: IPushRule | undefined;\n    67→    try {\n    68→        roomRule = client.getRoomPushRule(\"global\", roomId);\n    69→    } catch {\n    70→        // Possible that the client doesn't have pushRules yet. If so, it\n    71→        // hasn't started either, so indicate that this room is not notifying.\n    72→        return null;\n    73→    }\n    74→\n    75→    // XXX: We have to assume the default is to notify for all messages\n    76→    // (in particular this will be 'wrong' for one to one rooms because\n    77→    // they will notify loudly for all messages)\n    78→    if (!roomRule?.enabled) return RoomNotifState.AllMessages;\n    79→\n    80→    // a mute at the room level will still allow mentions\n    81→    // to notify\n    82→    if (isMuteRule(roomRule)) return RoomNotifState.MentionsOnly;\n    83→\n    84→    const actionsObject = PushProcessor.actionListToActionsObject(roomRule.actions);\n    85→    if (actionsObject.tweaks.sound) return RoomNotifState.AllMessagesLoud;\n    86→\n    87→    return null;\n    88→}\n    89→\n    90→export function setRoomNotifsState(client: MatrixClient, roomId: string, newState: RoomNotifState): Promise<void> {\n    91→    if (newState === RoomNotifState.Mute) {\n    92→        return setRoomNotifsStateMuted(client, roomId);\n    93→    } else {\n    94→        return setRoomNotifsStateUnmuted(client, roomId, newState);\n    95→    }\n    96→}\n    97→\n    98→export function getUnreadNotificationCount(\n    99→    room: Room,\n   100→    type: NotificationCountType,\n   101→    includeThreads: boolean,\n   102→    threadId?: string,\n   103→): number {\n   104→    const getCountShownForRoom = (r: Room, type: NotificationCountType): number => {\n   105→        return includeThreads ? r.getUnreadNotificationCount(type) : r.getRoomUnreadNotificationCount(type);\n   106→    };\n   107→\n   108→    let notificationCount = !!threadId\n   109→        ? room.getThreadUnreadNotificationCount(threadId, type)\n   110→        : getCountShownForRoom(room, type);\n   111→\n   112→    // Check notification counts in the old room just in case there's some lost\n   113→    // there. We only go one level down to avoid performance issues, and theory\n   114→    // is that 1st generation rooms will have already been read by the 3rd generation.\n   115→    const msc3946ProcessDynamicPredecessor = SettingsStore.getValue(\"feature_dynamic_room_predecessors\");\n   116→    const predecessor = room.findPredecessor(msc3946ProcessDynamicPredecessor);\n   117→    // Exclude threadId, as the same thread can't continue over a room upgrade\n   118→    if (!threadId && predecessor?.roomId) {\n   119→        const oldRoomId = predecessor.roomId;\n   120→        const oldRoom = room.client.getRoom(oldRoomId);\n   121→        if (oldRoom) {\n   122→            // We only ever care if there's highlights in the old room. No point in\n   123→            // notifying the user for unread messages because they would have extreme\n   124→            // difficulty changing their notification preferences away from \"All Messages\"\n   125→            // and \"Noisy\".\n   126→            notificationCount += getCountShownForRoom(oldRoom, NotificationCountType.Highlight);\n   127→        }\n   128→    }\n   129→\n   130→    return notificationCount;\n   131→}\n   132→\n   133→function setRoomNotifsStateMuted(cli: MatrixClient, roomId: string): Promise<any> {\n   134→    const promises: Promise<unknown>[] = [];\n   135→\n   136→    // delete the room rule\n   137→    const roomRule = cli.getRoomPushRule(\"global\", roomId);\n   138→    if (roomRule) {\n   139→        promises.push(cli.deletePushRule(\"global\", PushRuleKind.RoomSpecific, roomRule.rule_id));\n   140→    }\n   141→\n   142→    // add/replace an override rule to squelch everything in this room\n   143→    // NB. We use the room ID as the name of this rule too, although this\n   144→    // is an override rule, not a room rule: it still pertains to this room\n   145→    // though, so using the room ID as the rule ID is logical and prevents\n   146→    // duplicate copies of the rule.\n   147→    promises.push(\n   148→        cli.addPushRule(\"global\", PushRuleKind.Override, roomId, {\n   149→            conditions: [\n   150→                {\n   151→                    kind: ConditionKind.EventMatch,\n   152→                    key: \"room_id\",\n   153→                    pattern: roomId,\n   154→                },\n   155→            ],\n   156→            actions: [PushRuleActionName.DontNotify],\n   157→        }),\n   158→    );\n   159→\n   160→    return Promise.all(promises);\n   161→}\n   162→\n   163→function setRoomNotifsStateUnmuted(cli: MatrixClient, roomId: string, newState: RoomNotifState): Promise<any> {\n   164→    const promises: Promise<unknown>[] = [];\n   165→\n   166→    const overrideMuteRule = findOverrideMuteRule(cli, roomId);\n   167→    if (overrideMuteRule) {\n   168→        promises.push(cli.deletePushRule(\"global\", PushRuleKind.Override, overrideMuteRule.rule_id));\n   169→    }\n   170→\n   171→    if (newState === RoomNotifState.AllMessages) {\n   172→        const roomRule = cli.getRoomPushRule(\"global\", roomId);\n   173→        if (roomRule) {\n   174→            promises.push(cli.deletePushRule(\"global\", PushRuleKind.RoomSpecific, roomRule.rule_id));\n   175→        }\n   176→    } else if (newState === RoomNotifState.MentionsOnly) {\n   177→        promises.push(\n   178→            cli.addPushRule(\"global\", PushRuleKind.RoomSpecific, roomId, {\n   179→                actions: [PushRuleActionName.DontNotify],\n   180→            }),\n   181→        );\n   182→    } else if (newState === RoomNotifState.AllMessagesLoud) {\n   183→        promises.push(\n   184→            cli.addPushRule(\"global\", PushRuleKind.RoomSpecific, roomId, {\n   185→                actions: [\n   186→                    PushRuleActionName.Notify,\n   187→                    {\n   188→                        set_tweak: TweakName.Sound,\n   189→                        value: \"default\",\n   190→                    },\n   191→                ],\n   192→            }),\n   193→        );\n   194→    }\n   195→\n   196→    return Promise.all(promises);\n   197→}\n   198→\n   199→function findOverrideMuteRule(cli: MatrixClient | undefined, roomId: string): IPushRule | null {\n   200→    if (!cli?.pushRules?.global?.override) {\n   201→        return null;\n   202→    }\n   203→    for (const rule of cli.pushRules.global.override) {\n   204→        if (rule.enabled && isRuleRoomMuteRuleForRoomId(roomId, rule)) {\n   205→            return rule;\n   206→        }\n   207→    }\n   208→    return null;\n   209→}\n   210→\n   211→/**\n   212→ * Checks if a given rule is a room mute rule as implemented by EW\n   213→ * - matches every event in one room (one condition that is an event match on roomId)\n   214→ * - silences notifications (one action that is `DontNotify`)\n   215→ * @param rule - push rule\n   216→ * @returns {boolean} - true when rule mutes a room\n   217→ */\n   218→export function isRuleMaybeRoomMuteRule(rule: IPushRule): boolean {\n   219→    return (\n   220→        // matches every event in one room\n   221→        rule.conditions?.length === 1 &&\n   222→        rule.conditions[0].kind === ConditionKind.EventMatch &&\n   223→        rule.conditions[0].key === \"room_id\" &&\n   224→        // silences notifications\n   225→        isMuteRule(rule)\n   226→    );\n   227→}\n   228→\n   229→/**\n   230→ * Checks if a given rule is a room mute rule as implemented by EW\n   231→ * @param roomId - id of room to match\n   232→ * @param rule - push rule\n   233→ * @returns {boolean} true when rule mutes the given room\n   234→ */\n   235→function isRuleRoomMuteRuleForRoomId(roomId: string, rule: IPushRule): boolean {\n   236→    if (!isRuleMaybeRoomMuteRule(rule)) {\n   237→        return false;\n   238→    }\n   239→    // isRuleMaybeRoomMuteRule checks this condition exists\n   240→    const cond = rule.conditions![0]!;\n   241→    return cond.pattern === roomId;\n   242→}\n   243→\n   244→function isMuteRule(rule: IPushRule): boolean {\n   245→    // DontNotify is equivalent to the empty actions array\n   246→    return (\n   247→        rule.actions.length === 0 || (rule.actions.length === 1 && rule.actions[0] === PushRuleActionName.DontNotify)\n   248→    );\n   249→}\n   250→\n   251→/**\n   252→ * Returns an object giving information about the unread state of a room or thread\n   253→ * @param room The room to query, or the room the thread is in\n   254→ * @param threadId The thread to check the unread state of, or undefined to query the main thread\n   255→ * @param includeThreads If threadId is undefined, true to include threads other than the main thread, or\n   256→ *   false to exclude them. Ignored if threadId is specified.\n   257→ * @returns\n   258→ */\n   259→export function determineUnreadState(\n   260→    room?: Room,\n   261→    threadId?: string,\n   262→    includeThreads?: boolean,\n   263→): { level: NotificationLevel; symbol: string | null; count: number; invited: boolean } {\n   264→    if (!room) {\n   265→        return { symbol: null, count: 0, level: NotificationLevel.None, invited: false };\n   266→    }\n   267→\n   268→    if (getUnsentMessages(room, threadId).length > 0) {\n   269→        return { symbol: \"!\", count: 1, level: NotificationLevel.Unsent, invited: false };\n   270→    }\n   271→\n   272→    if (getEffectiveMembership(room.getMyMembership()) === EffectiveMembership.Invite) {\n   273→        return { symbol: \"!\", count: 1, level: NotificationLevel.Highlight, invited: true };\n   274→    }\n   275→\n   276→    if (SettingsStore.getValue(\"feature_ask_to_join\") && isKnockDenied(room)) {\n   277→        return { symbol: \"!\", count: 1, level: NotificationLevel.Highlight, invited: false };\n   278→    }\n   279→\n   280→    if (getRoomNotifsState(room.client, room.roomId) === RoomNotifState.Mute) {\n   281→        return { symbol: null, count: 0, level: NotificationLevel.None, invited: false };\n   282→    }\n   283→\n   284→    const redNotifs = getUnreadNotificationCount(\n   285→        room,\n   286→        NotificationCountType.Highlight,\n   287→        includeThreads ?? false,\n   288→        threadId,\n   289→    );\n   290→    const greyNotifs = getUnreadNotificationCount(room, NotificationCountType.Total, includeThreads ?? false, threadId);\n   291→\n   292→    const trueCount = greyNotifs || redNotifs;\n   293→    if (redNotifs > 0) {\n   294→        return { symbol: null, count: trueCount, level: NotificationLevel.Highlight, invited: false };\n   295→    }\n   296→\n   297→    const markedUnreadState = getMarkedUnreadState(room);\n   298→    if (greyNotifs > 0 || markedUnreadState) {\n   299→        return { symbol: null, count: trueCount, level: NotificationLevel.Notification, invited: false };\n   300→    }\n   301→\n   302→    // We don't have any notified messages, but we might have unread messages. Let's find out.\n   303→    let hasUnread = false;\n   304→    if (threadId) {\n   305→        const thread = room.getThread(threadId);\n   306→        if (thread) {\n   307→            hasUnread = doesRoomOrThreadHaveUnreadMessages(thread);\n   308→        }\n   309→        // If the thread does not exist, assume it contains no unreads\n   310→    } else {\n   311→        hasUnread = doesRoomHaveUnreadMessages(room, includeThreads ?? false);\n   312→    }\n   313→\n   314→    return {\n   315→        symbol: null,\n   316→        count: trueCount,\n   317→        level: hasUnread ? msc3946ProcessDynamicPredecessor, settingTACOnlyNotifs]);\n    44→\n    45→    const scheduleUpdate = useMemo(\n    46→        () =>\n    47→            throttle(doUpdate, MIN_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS, {\n    48→                leading: false,\n    49→                trailing: true,\n    50→            }),\n    51→        [doUpdate],\n    52→    );\n    53→\n    54→    // Listen to sync events to update and may change notifications.\n    58→    useEventEmitter(mxClient, MatrixEventEvent.Decrypted, scheduleUpdate);\n    59→\n    60→    // Force the list computation\n    61→    useEffect(() => {\n    62→        if (forceComputation) {\n    63→            doUpdate();\n    64→        }\n    65→    }, [doUpdate, forceComputation]);\n    66→\n    67→    return result;\n    68→}\n    69→\n    70→/**\n settingTACOnlyNotifs: boolean,\n    79→): Result {\n    80→    // Only count visible rooms to not torment the user with notification counts in rooms they can't see.\n    81→    // This will include with unread threads\n    89→        if (VisibilityProvider.instance.isRoomVisible(room) && doesRoomHaveUnreadThreads(room)) {\n    90→            // Get the greatest notification level of all threads\n    91→            const notificationLevel = getThreadNotificationLevel(room);\n    92→\n    93→            // If the room has an activity notification or less, we ignore it\n    94→ greatestNotificationLevel) {\n    99→                greatestNotificationLevel = notificationLevel;\n   100→            }\n   101→\n   102→            rooms.push({ room, notificationLevel });\n   103→        }\n   104→    }\n   105→\n   106→    const sortedRooms = rooms.sort((a, b) => sortRoom(a, b));\n   107→    return { greatestNotificationLevel, rooms: sortedRooms };\n   108→}\n   109→\n   110→/**\n   111→ * Store the room and its thread notification level\n   112→ */\n   113→type RoomData = Result[\"rooms\"][0];\n   114→\n   115→/**\n   116→ * Sort notification level by the most important notification level to the least important\n   117→ * Highlight > Notification > Activity\n   118→ * If the notification level is the same, we sort by the most recent thread\n   119→ * @param roomDataA - room and notification level of room A\n   120→ * @param roomDataB - room and notification level of room B\n   121→ * @returns {number}\n   122→ */\n   123→function sortRoom(roomDataA: RoomData, roomDataB: RoomData): number {\n   124→    const { notificationLevel: notificationLevelA, room: roomA } = roomDataA;\n   125→    const { notificationLevel: notificationLevelB, room: roomB } = roomDataB;\n   126→\n   127→    const timestampA = roomA.getLastThread()?.events.at(-1)?.getTs();\n   128→    const timestampB = roomB.getLastThread()?.events.at(-1)?.getTs();\n   129→\n   130→    // NotificationLevel is a numeric enum, so we can compare them directly\n   131→    if (notificationLevelA > notificationLevelB) return -1;\n   132→    else if (notificationLevelB > notificationLevelA) return 1;\n   133→    // Display most recent first\n   134→    else if (!timestampA) return 1;\n   135→    else if (!timestampB) return -1;\n   136→    else return timestampB - timestampA;\n   137→}\n   138→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01WfgRPF87x6MG9WCktYTUhA","type":"tool_result","content":"/*\nCopyright 2024 New Vector Ltd.\nCopyright 2015-2023 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\n\nSPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-only OR GPL-3.0-only OR LicenseRef-Element-Commercial\nPlease see LICENSE files in the repository root for full details.\n*/\n\nimport { M_BEACON, type Room, Thread, type MatrixEvent, EventType, type MatrixClient } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/matrix\";\nimport { logger } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/logger\";\n\nimport shouldHideEvent from \"./shouldHideEvent\";\nimport { haveRendererForEvent } from \"./events/EventTileFactory\";\nimport { RoomNotifState, getRoomNotifsState } from \"./RoomNotifs\";\n\n/**\n * Returns true if this event arriving in a room should affect the room's\n * count of unread messages\n *\n * @param client The Matrix Client instance of the logged-in user\n * @param {Object} ev The event\n * @returns {boolean} True if the given event should affect the unread message count\n */\nexport function eventTriggersUnreadCount(client: MatrixClient, ev: MatrixEvent): boolean {\n    if (ev.getSender() === client.getSafeUserId()) {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    switch (ev.getType()) {\n        case EventType.RoomMember:\n        case EventType.RoomThirdPartyInvite:\n        case EventType.CallAnswer:\n        case EventType.CallHangup:\n        case EventType.RoomCanonicalAlias:\n        case EventType.RoomServerAcl:\n        case M_BEACON.name:\n        case M_BEACON.altName:\n            return false;\n    }\n\n    if (ev.isRedacted()) return false;\n    try {\n        return haveRendererForEvent(ev, client, false /* hidden messages should never trigger unread counts anyways */);\n    } catch (e) {\n        console.warn(\"Error determining if event should trigger unread count\", e);\n        return false; // If we can't determine if the event should trigger an unread count, assume it does not.\n    }\n}\n\nexport function doesRoomHaveUnreadMessages(room: Room, includeThreads: boolean): boolean {\n    const toCheck: Array<Room | Thread> = [room];\n    if (includeThreads) {\n        toCheck.push(...room.getThreads());\n    }\n\n    for (const withTimeline of toCheck) {\n        if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, withTimeline.timeline)) {\n            // We found an unread, so the room is unread\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we got here then no timelines were found with unread messages.\n    return false;\n}\n\nfunction doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room: Room, timeline: Array<MatrixEvent>): boolean {\n    // The room is a space, let's ignore it\n    if (room.isSpaceRoom()) return false;\n\n    const myUserId = room.client.getSafeUserId();\n    const latestImportantEventId = findLatestImportantEvent(room.client, timeline)?.getId();\n    if (latestImportantEventId) {\n        return !room.hasUserReadEvent(myUserId, latestImportantEventId);\n    } else {\n        // We couldn't find an important event to check - check the unimportant ones.\n        const earliestUnimportantEventId = timeline.at(0)?.getId();\n        if (!earliestUnimportantEventId) {\n            // There are no events in this timeline - it is uninitialised, so we\n            // consider it read\n            return false;\n        } else if (room.hasUserReadEvent(myUserId, earliestUnimportantEventId)) {\n            // Some of the unimportant events are read, and there are no\n            // important ones after them, so we've read everything.\n            return false;\n        } else {\n            // We have events. and none of them are read.  We must guess that\n            // the timeline is unread, because there could be older unread\n            // important events that we don't have loaded.\n            logger.warn(\"Falling back to unread room because of no read receipt or counting message found\", {\n                roomId: room.roomId,\n                earliestUnimportantEventId: earliestUnimportantEventId,\n            });\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/**\n * Returns true if this room has unread threads.\n * @param room The room to check\n * @returns {boolean} True if the given room has unread threads\n */\nexport function doesRoomHaveUnreadThreads(room: Room): boolean {\n    if (getRoomNotifsState(room.client, room.roomId) === RoomNotifState.Mute) {\n        // No unread for muted rooms, nor their threads\n        // NB. This logic duplicated in RoomNotifs.determineUnreadState\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    for (const thread of room.getThreads()) {\n        if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, thread.timeline)) {\n            // We found an unread, so the room has an unread thread\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we got here then no threads were found with unread messages.\n    return false;\n}","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Vd2jy3Jq58NcH4PvDNj6MF","session_id":"bb9fc71c-8640-4602-bdd0-cecb051b7494","uuid":"f30d2985-8a64-499a-84ba-1a51ccd65943"}

2015-2023 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\n     4→\n     5→SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-only OR GPL-3.0-only OR LicenseRef-Element-Commercial\n     6→Please see LICENSE files in the repository root for full details.\n     7→*/\n     8→\n     9→import { M_BEACON, type Room, Thread, type MatrixEvent, EventType, type MatrixClient } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/matrix\";\n    10→import { logger } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/logger\";\n    11→\n    12→import shouldHideEvent from \"./shouldHideEvent\";\n    13→import { haveRendererForEvent } from \"./events/EventTileFactory\";\n    14→import { RoomNotifState, getRoomNotifsState } from \"./RoomNotifs\";\n    15→\n    16→/**\n    17→ * Returns true if this event arriving in a room should affect the room's\n    18→ * count of unread messages\n    19→ *\n    20→ * @param client The Matrix Client instance of the logged-in user\n    21→ * @param {Object} ev The event\n    22→ * @returns {boolean} True if the given event should affect the unread message count\n    23→ */\n    24→export function eventTriggersUnreadCount(client: MatrixClient, ev: MatrixEvent): boolean {\n    25→    if (ev.getSender() === client.getSafeUserId()) {\n    26→        return false;\n    27→    }\n    28→\n    29→    switch (ev.getType()) {\n    30→        case EventType.RoomMember:\n    31→        case EventType.RoomThirdPartyInvite:\n    32→        case EventType.CallAnswer:\n    33→        case EventType.CallHangup:\n    34→        case EventType.RoomCanonicalAlias:\n    35→        case EventType.RoomServerAcl:\n    36→        case M_BEACON.name:\n    37→        case M_BEACON.altName:\n    38→            return false;\n    39→    }\n    40→\n    41→    if (ev.isRedacted()) return false;\n    42→    try {\n    43→        return haveRendererForEvent(ev, client, false /* hidden messages should never trigger unread counts anyways */);\n    44→    } catch (e) {\n    45→        console.warn(\"Error determining if event should trigger unread count\", e);\n    46→        return false; // If we can't determine if the event should trigger an unread count, assume it does not.\n    47→    }\n    48→}\n    49→\n    50→/**\n    51→ * Checks if a user has read a specific event, optionally filtered by thread.\n    52→ *\n    53→ * @param room The room containing the event\n    54→ * @param userId The user ID to check\n    55→ * @param eventId The event ID to check\n    56→ * @param threadId Optional thread ID for thread-scoped receipts\n    57→ * @returns True if the user has read the event in the appropriate context\n    58→ */\n    59→function hasUserReadEvent(room: Room, userId: string, eventId: string, threadId?: string): boolean {\n    60→    // For thread timelines, we need to check for receipts with matching thread_id\n    61→    // For the main timeline, we check for receipts without thread_id or with thread_id=\"main\"\n    62→\n    63→    // Access the room's internal receipt storage\n    64→    // This is structured as: { eventId: { receiptType: { userId: { ts, thread_id? } } } }\n    65→    const roomReceipts = (room as any)._receipts;\n    66→    if (!roomReceipts || !roomReceipts[eventId]) {\n    67→        return false;\n    68→    }\n    69→\n    70→    const receiptsByType = roomReceipts[eventId];\n    71→\n    72→    // Check all receipt types (m.read, m.read.private, etc.)\n    73→    for (const receiptType in receiptsByType) {\n    74→        const receiptData = receiptsByType[receiptType];\n    75→        if (receiptData && receiptData[userId]) {\n    76→            const userReceipt = receiptData[userId];\n    77→            // Check if this receipt matches the thread context we're looking for\n    78→            if (threadId !== undefined) {\n    79→                // We're checking a thread - receipt must have matching thread_id\n    80→                if (userReceipt.thread_id === threadId) {\n    81→                    return true;\n    82→                }\n    83→            } else {\n    84→                // We're checking the main timeline - receipt should not have thread_id\n    85→                // or should have thread_id = \"main\"\n    86→                if (!userReceipt.thread_id || userReceipt.thread_id === \"main\") {\n    87→                    return true;\n    88→                }\n    89→            }\n    90→        }\n    91→    }\n    92→\n    93→    return false;\n    94→}\n    95→\n    96→export function doesRoomHaveUnreadMessages(room: Room, includeThreads: boolean): boolean {\n    97→    const toCheck: Array<[Room | Thread, string | undefined]> = [[room, undefined]];\n    98→    if (includeThreads) {\n    99→        for (const thread of room.getThreads()) {\n   100→            toCheck.push([thread, thread.id]);\n   101→        }\n   102→    }\n   103→\n   104→    for (const [withTimeline, threadId] of toCheck) {\n   105→        if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, withTimeline.timeline, threadId)) {\n   106→            // We found an unread, so the room is unread\n   107→            return true;\n   108→        }\n   109→    }\n   110→\n   111→    // If we got here then no timelines were found with unread messages.\n   112→    return false;\n   113→}\n   114→\n   115→function doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room: Room, timeline: Array<MatrixEvent>, threadId?: string): boolean {\n   116→    // The room is a space, let's ignore it\n   117→    if (room.isSpaceRoom()) return false;\n   118→\n   119→    const myUserId = room.client.getSafeUserId();\n   120→    const latestImportantEventId = findLatestImportantEvent(room.client, timeline)?.getId();\n   121→    if (latestImportantEventId) {\n   122→        return !hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, latestImportantEventId, threadId);\n   123→    } else {\n   124→        // We couldn't find an important event to check - check the unimportant ones.\n   125→        const earliestUnimportantEventId = timeline.at(0)?.getId();\n   126→        if (!earliestUnimportantEventId) {\n   127→            // There are no events in this timeline - it is uninitialised, so we\n   128→            // consider it read\n   129→            return false;\n   130→        } else if (hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, earliestUnimportantEventId, threadId)) {\n   131→            // Some of the unimportant events are read, and there are no\n   132→            // important ones after them, so we've read everything.\n   133→            return false;\n   134→        } else {\n   135→            // We have events. and none of them are read.  We must guess that\n   136→            // the timeline is unread, because there could be older unread\n   137→            // important events that we don't have loaded.\n   138→            logger.warn(\"Falling back to unread room because of no read receipt or counting message found\", {\n   139→                roomId: room.roomId,\n   140→                earliestUnimportantEventId: earliestUnimportantEventId,\n   141→            });\n   142→            return true;\n   143→        }\n   144→    }\n   145→}\n   146→\n   147→/**\n   148→ * Returns true if this room has unread threads.\n   149→ * @param room The room to check\n   150→ * @returns {boolean} True if the given room has unread threads\n   151→ */\n   152→export function doesRoomHaveUnreadThreads(room: Room): boolean {\n   153→    if (getRoomNotifsState(room.client, room.roomId) === RoomNotifState.Mute) {\n   154→        // No unread for muted rooms, nor their threads\n   155→        // NB. This logic duplicated in RoomNotifs.determineUnreadState\n   156→        return false;\n   157→    }\n   158→\n   159→    for (const thread of room.getThreads()) {\n   160→        if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, thread.timeline, thread.id)) {\n   161→            // We found an unread, so the room has an unread thread\n   162→            return true;\n   163→        }\n   164→    }\n   165→\n   166→    // If we got here then no threads were found with unread messages.\n   167→    return false;\n   168→}\n   169→\n   170→export function doesRoomOrThreadHaveUnreadMessages(roomOrThread: Room | Thread): boolean {\n   171→    const room = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.room : roomOrThread;\n   172→    const events = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.timeline : room.getLiveTimeline().getEvents();\n   173→    const threadId = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.id : undefined;\n   174→    return doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, events, threadId);\n   175→}\n   176→\n   177→/**\n   178→ * Look backwards through the timeline and find the last event that is\n   179→ * \"important\" in the sense of isImportantEvent.\n   180→ *\n   181→ * @returns the latest important event, or null if none were found\n   182→ */\n   183→function findLatestImportantEvent(client: MatrixClient, timeline: Array<MatrixEvent>): MatrixEvent | null {\n   184→    for (let index = timeline.length - 1; index >= 0; index--) {\n   185→        const event = timeline[index];\n   186→        if (isImportantEvent(client, event)) {\n   187→            return event;\n   188→        }\n   189→    }\n   190→    return null;\n   191→}\n   192→\n   193→/**\n   194→ * Given this event does not have a receipt, is it important enough to make\n   195→ * this room unread?\n   196→ */\n   197→function isImportantEvent(client: MatrixClient, event: MatrixEvent): boolean {\n   198→    return !shouldHideEvent(event) && eventTriggersUnreadCount(client, event);\n   199→}\n   200→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01E9VmcBZcEa94jZX7LLxFPX","session_id":"bb9fc71c-8640-4602-bdd0-cecb051b7494","uuid":"75cde159-2d67-4236-bfb5-2facca3446d4"}

Let me create a comprehensive analysis report.\n\n## Efficiency Review: Unread.ts Git Diff Analysis\n\nBased on my examination of the git diff and codebase, here's my detailed efficiency analysis:\n\n### **SUMMARY OF CHANGES**\n\nThe diff introduces:\n1. New `hasUserReadEvent()` function (lines 59-94) that replaces `room.hasUserReadEvent()` calls\n2. Modified `doesRoomHaveUnreadMessages()` to use tuples `[Room | Thread, string | undefined]` instead of simple array\n3. Thread ID threaded through multiple functions: `doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages()`, `doesRoomHaveUnreadThreads()`, and `doesRoomOrThreadHaveUnreadMessages()`\n\n---\n\n### **EFFICIENCY ISSUES FOUND**\n\n#### **1. N+1 Receipt Type Iteration (MODERATE CONCERN)**\n\n**Issue:** The new `hasUserReadEvent()` function iterates through ALL receipt types for every receipt lookup:\n\n```typescript\n// Line 73-91: Iterates through every receipt type\nfor (const receiptType in receiptsByType) {\n    const receiptData = receiptsByType[receiptType];\n    if (receiptData && receiptData[userId]) {\n        const userReceipt = receiptData[userId];\n        if (threadId !== undefined) {\n            if (userReceipt.thread_id === threadId) {\n                return true;  // Early return\n            }\n        } else {\n            if (!userReceipt.thread_id || userReceipt.thread_id === \"main\") {\n                return true;  // Early return\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Analysis:**\n- The loop has early returns, which is **good** (short-circuits after finding a match)\n- However, it will typically check `m.read`, `m.read.private`, and potentially other receipt types\n- In a typical scenario with 2-3 receipt types, this isn't catastrophic\n- The original `room.hasUserReadEvent()` likely had similar iteration logic internally\n\n**Recommendation:** This is acceptable given the early returns, but consider checking the matrix-js-sdk implementation to see if there's a more direct access pattern.\n\n---\n\n#### **2. Redundant Array Tuple Overhead (LOW CONCERN)**\n\n**Issue:** Changed from simple array spread to loop with tuple construction:\n\n```typescript\n// OLD (lines 50-51):\nconst toCheck: Array<Room | Thread> = [room];\nif (includeThreads) {\n    toCheck.push(...room.getThreads());\n}\n\n// NEW (lines 97-102):\nconst toCheck: Array<[Room | Thread, string | undefined]> = [[room, undefined]];\nif (includeThreads) {\n    for (const thread of room.getThreads()) {\n        toCheck.push([thread, thread.id]);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Analysis:**\n- Creating tuples is marginally slower than simple array push\n- The original spread operator was cleaner: `toCheck.push(...room.getThreads())`\n- Now explicit loop creates tuples for each thread\n- **Memory impact:** Negligible. Each tuple is just two pointers per thread\n- **Performance impact:** Negligible. The loop is O(n) where n = number of threads (typically 0-20)\n\n**Recommendation:** This is a minor readability/performance trade-off, but acceptable since:\n- It makes thread ID passing explicit\n- It passes information alongside data rather than looking it up later\n- It avoids potential bugs from thread ID lookup\n\n---\n\n#### **3. Thread ID Lookup Deferred (POTENTIAL ADVANTAGE)**\n\n**Positive finding:** The old code would have looked up `thread.id` inside `doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages()` each time:\n\n```typescript\n// OLD implicit pattern (thread was an object, you'd need thread.id later)\nfor (const withTimeline of toCheck) {\n    // Inside doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages, you'd need to determine thread ID somehow\n    if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, withTimeline.timeline)) { ... }\n}\n```\n\n**New approach:**\n```typescript\n// NEW explicit pattern\nfor (const [withTimeline, threadId] of toCheck) {\n    if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, withTimeline.timeline, threadId)) { ... }\n}\n```\n\nThis avoids needing to re-determine if something is a Thread and what its ID is. **This is actually better.**\n\n---\n\n#### **4. Hot Path Analysis - CRITICAL FINDING**\n\nLooking at call locations:\n\n**Hot paths (called frequently):**\n1. **`determineUnreadState()` in RoomNotifs.ts:259** - Called for EVERY room notification calculation\n   - Called from notification UI updates\n   - Called during syncs and when events are processed\n   - **This is a very hot path** - called potentially 100s of times per minute in active clients\n\n2. **`useUnreadThreadRooms()` in useUnreadThreadRooms.ts:34** - Called on sync and decryption\n   - Iterates ALL visible rooms\n   - Calls `doesRoomHaveUnreadThreads()` for each room\n   - Throttled to 500ms but still called frequently\n\n3. **Notifications.tsx:807** - Called when rendering notification settings\n   - Iterates all rooms: `.getRooms().some((r) => doesRoomHaveUnreadMessages(r, true))`\n   - Not as hot, but still called during UI render\n\n**Performance implications:**\n\nThe `hasUserReadEvent()` function is called **twice per room check**:\n```typescript\n// In doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages (lines 122, 130):\nreturn !hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, latestImportantEventId, threadId);  // Call 1\n...\nelse if (hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, earliestUnimportantEventId, threadId)) { // Call 2\n```\n\nIn a hot path like `determineUnreadState()` being called 100+ times/minute across 100+ rooms = **10,000+ receipt lookups per minute.**\n\n---\n\n#### **5. Caching Opportunity - NOT IMPLEMENTED (MAJOR INEFFICIENCY)**\n\n**Critical inefficiency:** `room._receipts` is accessed directly but `myUserId` is fetched fresh in every call:\n\n```typescript\nconst myUserId = room.client.getSafeUserId();  // Called for every timeline check\nconst latestImportantEventId = findLatestImportantEvent(...)?. getId();\nif (latestImportantEventId) {\n    return !hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, latestImportantEventId, threadId);\n}\n```\n\nIf a room has 5 threads, `doesRoomHaveUnreadMessages(room, true)` calls:\n- `findLatestImportantEvent()` potentially 6 times (main + 5 threads)\n- `myUserId = room.client.getSafeUserId()` 6 times\n- `hasUserReadEvent()` 6-12 times (up to 2 per timeline)\n\n**The same `myUserId` is fetched 6+ times in a single function call.**\n\n---\n\n#### **6. `findLatestImportantEvent()` Called Multiple Times (INEFFICIENCY)**\n\n**Issue:** For each timeline, the function scans backwards:\n\n```typescript\n// In doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages (line 120)\nconst latestImportantEventId = findLatestImportantEvent(room.client, timeline)?.getId();\n```\n\nFor a thread room:\n- Main timeline scan: worst case O(timeline.length)\n- Thread 1 timeline scan: worst case O(timeline.length)\n- ... × N threads\n\nNo caching of results.\n\n---\n\n### **DETAILED ASSESSMENT BY CRITERIA**\n\n| Criterion | Status | Notes |\n|-----------|--------|-------|\n| **N+1 Receipt Type Loops** | ACCEPTABLE | Early returns prevent most iterations |\n| **Tuple Array Overhead** | LOW | 2-3 threads typical, negligible memory/time |\n| **Repeated Work Caching** | **RED FLAG** | `getSafeUserId()` called 6+ times per check |\n| **Early Returns/Short-Circuit** | GOOD | `hasUserReadEvent()` returns immediately on match |\n| **Hot Path Frequency** | **CRITICAL** | Called 100+ times/min across all rooms |\n| **Memory Efficiency** | ACCEPTABLE | Tuples are minimal overhead |\n\n---\n\n### **RECOMMENDATIONS**\n\n**Priority 1 (High Impact):**\n1. **Cache `myUserId` at the top of `doesRoomHaveUnreadMessages()`**\n   ```typescript\n   export function doesRoomHaveUnreadMessages(room: Room, includeThreads: boolean): boolean {\n       const myUserId = room.client.getSafeUserId();  // Cache once\n       const toCheck: Array<[Room | Thread, string | undefined]> = [[room, undefined]];\n       // ... pass myUserId to doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages\n   }\n   ```\n\n2. **Pass `myUserId` through the call chain** rather than fetching it in `doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages()`\n\n**Priority 2 (Medium Impact):**\n1. Consider memoizing `findLatestImportantEvent()` results per timeline (if timelines are immutable)\n2. Profile `room._receipts` access patterns - ensure you're accessing the right structure\n\n**Priority 3 (Minor):**\n1. Document the `_receipts` internal structure since you're accessing private API\n2. Consider caching `hasUserReadEvent()` results per event ID if the function is called with the same parameters frequently\n\n---\n\n### **CONCLUSION**\n\nThe new `hasUserReadEvent()` function itself is reasonably efficient with good early returns. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01R1x36wHrBSWG1kiyPAS8dM","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my findings.\n\n## Analysis Report: Thread-Aware Receipt Checking in `apps/web/src/Unread.ts`\n\nBased on my comprehensive search of the codebase, here are my findings:\n\n### 1. **New `hasUserReadEvent()` Function Analysis**\n\nThe new function implements **custom thread-aware receipt checking** by directly accessing the private `_receipts` property of the Room object. This is notable because:\n\n- **No existing SDK alternative**: The matrix-js-sdk does not provide built-in support for thread-scoped receipt checking. The only public API available is `room.getReceiptsForEvent(event)`, which returns receipts without thread context.\n- **Only implementation in codebase**: This is the **only place in the entire Element Web codebase** where `_receipts` is directly accessed.\n- **Why it's necessary**: The matrix-js-sdk's `hasUserReadEvent()` method (if it exists on the Room object) doesn't support thread filtering, which is why a custom implementation was created.\n\n### 2. **Existing Receipt Checking Patterns**\n\n**In `MessagePanel.tsx` (lines 878-915)**:\n```typescript\n// Uses public API: getReceiptsForEvent()\nreceiptDestination.getReceiptsForEvent(event).forEach((r) => {\n    if (!r.userId || !isSupportedReceiptType(r.type) || r.userId === myUserId) {\n        return; // ignore non-read receipts and receipts from self.\n    }\n    // ...\n});\n```\n\nThis pattern:\n- Uses `getReceiptsForEvent()` - a public API on both Room and Thread objects\n- Already handles thread-aware receipt retrieval by checking `this.context.threadId`\n- Gets receipts **for a specific event**, not checking if a user read a specific event\n\n**Key difference**: `MessagePanel.tsx` retrieves ALL receipts for an event, while the new `hasUserReadEvent()` checks if a **specific user** read a **specific event** in a **specific thread context**.\n\n### 3. **Thread-ID Semantics**\n\nFrom the test file and code analysis, the receipt system uses:\n- **No `thread_id` or `thread_id: \"main\"`** → Main timeline receipt\n- **`thread_id: <thread_root_id>`** → Thread-scoped receipt for a specific thread\n- This is confirmed in `/workspace/apps/web/test/unit-tests/Unread-test.ts` (lines 233, 297, 337, 482-483, 591)\n\n### 4. **Receipt Data Structure**\n\nThe `_receipts` property is structured as:\n```typescript\n{\n  [eventId]: {\n    [receiptType]: {        // e.g., \"m.read\", \"m.read.private\"\n      [userId]: {\n        ts: number,\n        thread_id?: string   // Optional: \"main\" or thread root ID\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\nThis matches the Matrix spec for thread-aware read receipts.\n\n### 5. **Files Involved with Receipt Handling**\n\n**Direct receipt handling**:\n- `/workspace/apps/web/src/Unread.ts` - arriving in a room should affect the room's\n * count of unread messages\n *\n * @param client The Matrix Client instance of the logged-in user\n * @param {Object} ev The event\n * @returns {boolean} True if the given event should affect the unread message count\n */\nexport function eventTriggersUnreadCount(client: MatrixClient, ev: MatrixEvent): boolean {\n    if (ev.getSender() === client.getSafeUserId()) {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    switch (ev.getType()) {\n        case EventType.RoomMember:\n        case EventType.RoomThirdPartyInvite:\n        case EventType.CallAnswer:\n        case EventType.CallHangup:\n        case EventType.RoomCanonicalAlias:\n        case EventType.RoomServerAcl:\n        case M_BEACON.name:\n        case M_BEACON.altName:\n            return false;\n    }\n\n    if (ev.isRedacted()) return false;\n    try {\n        return haveRendererForEvent(ev, client, false /* hidden messages should never trigger unread counts anyways */);\n    } catch (e) {\n        console.warn(\"Error determining if event should trigger unread count\", e);\n        return false; // If we can't determine if the event should trigger an unread count, assume it does not.\n    }\n}\n\n/**\n * Checks if a user has read a specific event, optionally filtered by thread.\n *\n * @param room The room containing the event\n * @param userId The user ID to check\n * @param eventId The event ID to check\n * @param threadId Optional thread ID for thread-scoped receipts\n * @returns True if the user has read the event in the appropriate context\n */\nfunction hasUserReadEvent(room: Room, userId: string, eventId: string, threadId?: string): boolean {\n    // For thread timelines, we need to check for receipts with matching thread_id\n    // For the main timeline, we check for receipts without thread_id or with thread_id=\"main\"\n\n    // Access the room's internal receipt storage\n    // This is structured as: { eventId: { receiptType: { userId: { ts, thread_id? } } } }\n    const roomReceipts = (room as any)._receipts;\n    if (!roomReceipts || !roomReceipts[eventId]) {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    const receiptsByType = roomReceipts[eventId];\n\n    // Check all receipt types (m.read, m.read.private, etc.)\n    for (const receiptType in receiptsByType) {\n        const receiptData = receiptsByType[receiptType];\n        if (receiptData && receiptData[userId]) {\n            const userReceipt = receiptData[userId];\n            // Check if this receipt matches the thread context we're looking for\n            if (threadId !== undefined) {\n                // We're checking a thread - receipt must have matching thread_id\n                if (userReceipt.thread_id === threadId) {\n                    return true;\n                }\n            } else {\n                // We're checking the main timeline - receipt should not have thread_id\n                // or should have thread_id = \"main\"\n                if (!userReceipt.thread_id || userReceipt.thread_id === \"main\") {\n                    return true;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    return false;\n}\n\nexport function doesRoomHaveUnreadMessages(room: Room, includeThreads: boolean): boolean {\n    const toCheck: Array<[Room | Thread, string | undefined]> = [[room, undefined]];\n    if (includeThreads) {\n        for (const thread of room.getThreads()) {\n            toCheck.push([thread, thread.id]);\n        }\n    }\n\n    for (const [withTimeline, threadId] of toCheck) {\n        if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, withTimeline.timeline, threadId)) {\n            // We found an unread, so the room is unread\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we got here then no timelines were found with unread messages.\n    return false;\n}\n\nfunction doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room: Room, timeline: Array<MatrixEvent>, threadId?: string): boolean {\n    // The room is a space, let's ignore it\n    if (room.isSpaceRoom()) return false;\n\n    const myUserId = room.client.getSafeUserId();\n    const latestImportantEventId = findLatestImportantEvent(room.client, timeline)?.getId();\n    if (latestImportantEventId) {\n        return !hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, latestImportantEventId, threadId);\n    } else {\n        // We couldn't find an important event to check - check the unimportant ones.\n        const earliestUnimportantEventId = timeline.at(0)?.getId();\n        if (!earliestUnimportantEventId) {\n            // There are no events in this timeline - it is uninitialised, so we\n            // consider it read\n            return false;\n        } else if (hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, earliestUnimportantEventId, threadId)) {\n            // Some of the unimportant events are read, and there are no\n            // important ones after them, so we've read everything.\n            return false;\n        } else {\n            // We have events. and none of them are read.  We must guess that\n            // the timeline is unread, because there could be older unread\n            // important events that we don't have loaded.\n            logger.warn(\"Falling back to unread room because of no read receipt or counting message found\", {\n                roomId: room.roomId,\n                earliestUnimportantEventId: earliestUnimportantEventId,\n            });\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/**\n * Returns true if this room has unread threads.\n * @param room The room to check\n * @returns {boolean} True if the given room has unread threads\n */\nexport function doesRoomHaveUnreadThreads(room: Room): boolean {\n    if (getRoomNotifsState(room.client, room.roomId) === RoomNotifState.Mute) {\n        // No unread for muted rooms, nor their threads\n        // NB. This logic duplicated in RoomNotifs.determineUnreadState\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    for (const thread of room.getThreads()) {\n        if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, thread.timeline, thread.id)) {\n            // We found an unread, so the room has an unread thread\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we got here then no threads were found with unread messages.\n    return false;\n}\n\nexport function doesRoomOrThreadHaveUnreadMessages(roomOrThread: Room | Thread): boolean {\n    const room = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.room : roomOrThread;\n    const events = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.timeline : room.getLiveTimeline().getEvents();\n    const threadId = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.id : undefined;\n    return doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, events, threadId);\n}\n\n/**\n * Look backwards through the timeline and find the last event that is\n * \"important\" in the sense of isImportantEvent.\n *\n * @returns the latest important event, or null if none were found\n */\nfunction findLatestImportantEvent(client: MatrixClient, timeline: Array<MatrixEvent>): MatrixEvent | null {\n    for (let index = timeline.length - 1; index >= 0; index--) {\n        const event = timeline[index];\n        if (isImportantEvent(client, event)) {\n            return event;\n        }\n    }\n    return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Given this event does not have a receipt, is it important enough to make\n * this room unread?\n */\nfunction isImportantEvent(client: MatrixClient, event: MatrixEvent): boolean {\n    return !shouldHideEvent(event) && eventTriggersUnreadCount(client, event);\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":7,"oldLines":12,"newStart":7,"newLines":16,"lines":[" */"," "," import { M_BEACON, type Room, Thread, type MatrixEvent, EventType, type MatrixClient } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/matrix\";","+import { isSupportedReceiptType } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/utils\";"," import { logger } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/logger\";"," "," import shouldHideEvent from \"./shouldHideEvent\";"," import { haveRendererForEvent } from \"./events/EventTileFactory\";"," import { RoomNotifState, getRoomNotifsState } from \"./RoomNotifs\";"," ","+// Constant for the main timeline thread ID in read receipts","+const MAIN_THREAD_ID = \"main\";","+"," /**","  * Returns true if this event arriving in a room should affect the room's","  * count of unread messages"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

thread_id or with thread_id=\"main\"\n\n    // Access the room's internal receipt storage\n    // This is structured as: { eventId: { receiptType: { userId: { ts, thread_id? } } } }\n    const roomReceipts = (room as any)._receipts;\n    if (!roomReceipts || !roomReceipts[eventId]) {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    const receiptsByType = roomReceipts[eventId];\n\n    // Check all receipt types (m.read, m.read.private, etc.)\n    for (const receiptType in receiptsByType) {\n        const receiptData = receiptsByType[receiptType];\n        if (receiptData && receiptData[userId]) {\n            const userReceipt = receiptData[userId];\n            // Check if this receipt matches the thread context we're looking for\n            if (threadId !== undefined) {\n                // We're checking a thread - receipt must have matching thread_id\n                if (userReceipt.thread_id === threadId) {\n                    return true;\n                }\n            } else {\n                // We're checking the main timeline - receipt should not have thread_id\n                // or should have thread_id = \"main\"\n                if (!userReceipt.thread_id || userReceipt.thread_id === \"main\") {\n                    return true;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    return false;\n}\n\nexport function doesRoomHaveUnreadMessages(room: Room, includeThreads: boolean): boolean {\n    const toCheck: Array<[Room | Thread, string | undefined]> = [[room, undefined]];\n    if (includeThreads) {\n        for (const thread of room.getThreads()) {\n            toCheck.push([thread, thread.id]);\n        }\n    }\n\n    for (const [withTimeline, threadId] of toCheck) {\n        if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, withTimeline.timeline, threadId)) {\n            // We found an unread, so the room is unread\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we got here then no timelines were found with unread messages.\n    return false;\n}\n\nfunction doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room: Room, timeline: Array<MatrixEvent>, threadId?: string): boolean {\n    // The room is a space, let's ignore it\n    if (room.isSpaceRoom()) return false;\n\n    const myUserId = room.client.getSafeUserId();\n    const latestImportantEventId = findLatestImportantEvent(room.client, timeline)?.getId();\n    if (latestImportantEventId) {\n        return !hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, latestImportantEventId, threadId);\n    } else {\n        // We couldn't find an important event to check - check the unimportant ones.\n        const earliestUnimportantEventId = timeline.at(0)?.getId();\n        if (!earliestUnimportantEventId) {\n            // There are no events in this timeline - it is uninitialised, so we\n            // consider it read\n            return false;\n        } else if (hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, earliestUnimportantEventId, threadId)) {\n            // Some of the unimportant events are read, and there are no\n            // important ones after them, so we've read everything.\n            return false;\n        } else {\n            // We have events. and none of them are read.  We must guess that\n            // the timeline is unread, because there could be older unread\n            // important events that we don't have loaded.\n            logger.warn(\"Falling back to unread room because of no read receipt or counting message found\", {\n                roomId: room.roomId,\n                earliestUnimportantEventId: earliestUnimportantEventId,\n            });\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/**\n * Returns true if this room has unread threads.\n * @param room The room to check\n * @returns {boolean} True if the given room has unread threads\n */\nexport function doesRoomHaveUnreadThreads(room: Room): boolean {\n    if (getRoomNotifsState(room.client, room.roomId) === RoomNotifState.Mute) {\n        // No unread for muted rooms, nor their threads\n        // NB. This logic duplicated in RoomNotifs.determineUnreadState\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    for (const thread of room.getThreads()) {\n        if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, thread.timeline, thread.id)) {\n            // We found an unread, so the room has an unread thread\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we got here then no threads were found with unread messages.\n    return false;\n}\n\nexport function doesRoomOrThreadHaveUnreadMessages(roomOrThread: Room | Thread): boolean {\n    const room = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.room : roomOrThread;\n    const events = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.timeline : room.getLiveTimeline().getEvents();\n    const threadId = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.id : undefined;\n    return doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, events, threadId);\n}\n\n/**\n * Look backwards through the timeline and find the last event that is\n * \"important\" in the sense of isImportantEvent.\n *\n * @returns the latest important event, or null if none were found\n */\nfunction findLatestImportantEvent(client: MatrixClient, timeline: Array<MatrixEvent>): MatrixEvent | null {\n    for (let index = timeline.length - 1; index >= 0; index--) {\n        const event = timeline[index];\n        if (isImportantEvent(client, event)) {\n            return event;\n        }\n    }\n    return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Given this event does not have a receipt, is it important enough to make\n * this room unread?\n */\nfunction isImportantEvent(client: MatrixClient, event: MatrixEvent): boolean {\n    return !shouldHideEvent(event) && eventTriggersUnreadCount(client, event);\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":54,"oldLines":6,"newStart":54,"newLines":9,"lines":[" /**","  * Checks if a user has read a specific event, optionally filtered by thread.","  *","+ * Note: This function accesses the private `_receipts` property because the matrix-js-sdk","+ * does not provide a public API that combines user receipt checking with thread-scoped filtering.","+ *","  * @param room The room containing the event","  * @param userId The user ID to check","  * @param eventId The event ID to check"]},{"oldStart":73,"oldLines":8,"newStart":76,"newLines":13,"lines":[" ","     const receiptsByType = roomReceipts[eventId];"," ","-    // Check all receipt types (m.read, m.read.private, etc.)","+    // Check all supported receipt types (m.read, m.read.private, etc.)","     for (const receiptType in receiptsByType) {","+        // Only consider supported receipt types","+        if (!isSupportedReceiptType(receiptType)) {","+            continue;","+        }","+","         const receiptData = receiptsByType[receiptType];","         if (receiptData && receiptData[userId]) {","             const userReceipt = receiptData[userId];"]},{"oldStart":86,"oldLines":8,"newStart":94,"newLines":8,"lines":["                 }","             } else {","                 // We're checking the main timeline - the unread message count\n */\nexport function eventTriggersUnreadCount(client: MatrixClient, ev: MatrixEvent): boolean {\n    if (ev.getSender() === client.getSafeUserId()) {\n        return false;\n case EventType.RoomCanonicalAlias:\n        case EventType.RoomServerAcl:\n        case M_BEACON.name:\n        case M_BEACON.altName:\n            return false;\n    }\n\n    if (ev.isRedacted()) @param threadId Optional thread ID for thread-scoped receipts\n * @returns True if the user has read the event in the appropriate context\n */\nfunction hasUserReadEvent(room: Room, userId: string, eventId: string, threadId?: string): boolean {\n    // For thread timelines, we need to check for receipts with matching thread_id\n    // For the main timeline, we check for receipts without thread_id or with thread_id=\"main\"\n\n    // Access the room's internal receipt storage\n    // This is structured as: { eventId: { receiptType: { userId: { ts, thread_id? } } } }\n    const roomReceipts = (room as any)._receipts;\n    if (!roomReceipts || !roomReceipts[eventId]) {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    const receiptsByType = roomReceipts[eventId];\n\n    // Check all supported receipt types (m.read, m.read.private, etc.)\n    for (const receiptType in receiptsByType) {\n        // Only consider supported receipt types\n        if (!isSupportedReceiptType(receiptType)) {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        const receiptData = receiptsByType[receiptType];\n        if (receiptData && receiptData[userId]) {\n userReceipt.thread_id === MAIN_THREAD_ID) {\n                    return true;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    return false;\n}\n\nexport function doesRoomHaveUnreadMessages(room: Room, includeThreads: boolean): boolean {\n    const toCheck: Array<[Room | Thread, string | undefined]> = [[room, undefined]];\n    if (includeThreads) {\n        for    // There are no events in this timeline - it is uninitialised, so we\n            // consider it read\n            return false;\n        } else if (hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, earliestUnimportantEventId, threadId)) {\n            // Some of the unimportant events are read, and there are no\n            // important ones after them, so we've read everything.\n            return false;\n        } else {\n            // We have events. and none of them are read.  We must guess that\n            // the timeline is unread, because there could be older unread\n            // important events that we don't have loaded.\n            logger.warn(\"Falling back to unread room because of no read receipt or counting message found\", {\n                roomId: room.roomId,\n                earliestUnimportantEventId: earliestUnimportantEventId,\n            });\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/**\n * Returns true if this room has unread threads.\n * @param room The room to check\n * @returns {boolean} True if the given room has unread threads\n */\nexport function doesRoomHaveUnreadThreads(room: Room): boolean {\n    if (getRoomNotifsState(room.client, room.roomId) === RoomNotifState.Mute) {\n        // No unread for muted rooms, nor their threads\n        // NB. We must guess that\n            // the timeline is unread, because there could be older unread\n            // important events that we don't have loaded.\n            logger.warn(\"Falling back to unread room because of no read receipt or counting message found\", {\n                roomId: room.roomId,\n                earliestUnimportantEventId: earliestUnimportantEventId,\n            });\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n}","originalFile":"/*\nCopyright 2024 New Vector Ltd.\nCopyright 2015-2023 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\n\nSPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-only OR GPL-3.0-only OR LicenseRef-Element-Commercial\nPlease see LICENSE files in the repository root for full details.\n*/\n\nimport { M_BEACON, type Room, Thread, type MatrixEvent, EventType, type MatrixClient } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/matrix\";\nimport { isSupportedReceiptType } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/utils\";\nimport { logger } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/logger\";\n\nimport shouldHideEvent from \"./shouldHideEvent\";\nimport { haveRendererForEvent } from \"./events/EventTileFactory\";\nimport { RoomNotifState, getRoomNotifsState } from \"./RoomNotifs\";\n\n// Constant for the main timeline thread ID in read receipts\nconst MAIN_THREAD_ID = \"main\";\n\n/**\n * Returns true if this event arriving in a room should affect the room's\n * count of unread messages\n *\n * @param client The Matrix Client instance of the logged-in user\n * @param {Object} ev The event\n * @returns {boolean} True if the given event should affect the unread message count\n */\nexport function eventTriggersUnreadCount(client: MatrixClient, ev: MatrixEvent): boolean {\n    if (ev.getSender() === client.getSafeUserId()) {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    switch (ev.getType()) {\n        case EventType.RoomMember:\n        case EventType.RoomThirdPartyInvite:\n        case EventType.CallAnswer:\n        case EventType.CallHangup:\n        case EventType.RoomCanonicalAlias:\n        case EventType.RoomServerAcl:\n        case M_BEACON.name:\n        case M_BEACON.altName:\n            return false;\n    }\n\n    if (ev.isRedacted()) return false;\n    try {\n        return haveRendererForEvent(ev, client, false /* hidden messages should never trigger unread counts anyways */);\n    } catch (e) {\n        console.warn(\"Error determining if event should trigger unread count\", e);\n        return false; // If we can't determine if the event should trigger an unread count, assume it does not.\n    }\n}\n\n/**\n * Checks if a user has read a specific event, optionally filtered by thread.\n *\n * Note: This function accesses the private `_receipts` property because the matrix-js-sdk\n * does not provide a public API that combines user receipt checking with thread-scoped filtering.\n *\n * @param room The room containing the event\n * @param userId The user ID to check\n * @param eventId The event ID to check\n * @param threadId Optional thread ID for thread-scoped receipts\n * @returns True if the user has read the event in the appropriate context\n */\nfunction hasUserReadEvent(room: Room, userId: string, eventId: string, threadId?: string): boolean {\n    // For thread timelines, we need to check for receipts with matching thread_id\n    // For the main timeline, we check for receipts without thread_id or with thread_id=\"main\"\n\n    // Access the room's internal receipt storage\n    // This is structured as: { eventId: { receiptType: { userId: { ts, thread_id? } } } }\n    const roomReceipts = (room as any)._receipts;\n    if (!roomReceipts || !roomReceipts[eventId]) {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    const receiptsByType = roomReceipts[eventId];\n\n    // Check all supported receipt types (m.read, m.read.private, etc.)\n    for (const receiptType in receiptsByType) {\n        // Only consider supported receipt types\n        if (!isSupportedReceiptType(receiptType)) {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        const receiptData = receiptsByType[receiptType];\n        if (receiptData && receiptData[userId]) {\n            const userReceipt = receiptData[userId];\n            // Check if this receipt matches the thread context we're looking for\n            if (threadId !== undefined) {\n                // We're checking a thread - receipt must have matching thread_id\n                if (userReceipt.thread_id === threadId) {\n                    return true;\n                }\n            } else {\n                // We're checking the main timeline - receipt should not have thread_id\n                // or should have thread_id = MAIN_THREAD_ID\n                if (!userReceipt.thread_id || userReceipt.thread_id === MAIN_THREAD_ID) {\n                    return true;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    return false;\n}\n\nexport function doesRoomHaveUnreadMessages(room: Room, includeThreads: boolean): boolean {\n    // Cache userId to avoid repeated lookups in the hot path\n    const myUserId = room.client.getSafeUserId();\n    const toCheck: Array<[Room | Thread, string | undefined]> = [[room, undefined]];\n    if (includeThreads) {\n        for (const thread of room.getThreads()) {\n            toCheck.push([thread, thread.id]);\n        }\n    }\n\n    for (const [withTimeline, threadId] of toCheck) {\n        if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, myUserId, withTimeline.timeline, threadId)) {\n            // We found an unread, so the room is unread\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we got here then no timelines were found with unread messages.\n    return false;\n}\n\nfunction doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room: Room, timeline: Array<MatrixEvent>, threadId?: string): boolean {\n    // The room is a space, let's ignore it\n    if (room.isSpaceRoom()) return false;\n\n    const myUserId = room.client.getSafeUserId();\n    const latestImportantEventId = findLatestImportantEvent(room.client, timeline)?.getId();\n    if (latestImportantEventId) {\n        return !hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, latestImportantEventId, threadId);\n    } else {\n        // We couldn't find an important event to check - check the unimportant ones.\n        const earliestUnimportantEventId = timeline.at(0)?.getId();\n        if (!earliestUnimportantEventId) {\n            // There are no events in this timeline - it is uninitialised, so we\n            // consider it read\n            return false;\n        } else if (hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, earliestUnimportantEventId, threadId)) {\n            // Some of the unimportant events are read, and there are no\n            // important ones after them, so we've read everything.\n            return false;\n        } else {\n            // We have events. and none of them are read.  We must guess that\n            // the timeline is unread, because there could be older unread\n            // important events that we don't have loaded.\n            logger.warn(\"Falling back to unread room because of no read receipt or counting message found\", {\n                roomId: room.roomId,\n                earliestUnimportantEventId: earliestUnimportantEventId,\n            });\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/**\n * Returns true if this room has unread threads.\n * @param room The room to check\n * @returns {boolean} True if the given room has unread threads\n */\nexport function doesRoomHaveUnreadThreads(room: Room): boolean {\n    if (getRoomNotifsState(room.client, room.roomId) === RoomNotifState.Mute) {\n        // No unread for muted rooms, nor their threads\n        // NB. This logic duplicated in RoomNotifs.determineUnreadState\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    for (const thread of room.getThreads()) {\n        if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, thread.timeline, thread.id)) {\n            // We found an unread, so the room has an unread thread\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we got here then no threads were found with unread messages.\n    return false;\n}\n\nexport function doesRoomOrThreadHaveUnreadMessages(roomOrThread: Room | Thread): boolean {\n    const room = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.room : roomOrThread;\n    const events = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.timeline : room.getLiveTimeline().getEvents();\n    const threadId = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.id : undefined;\n    return doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, events, threadId);\n}\n\n/**\n * Look backwards through the timeline and find the last event that is\n * \"important\" in the sense of isImportantEvent.\n *\n * @returns the latest important event, or null if none were found\n */\nfunction findLatestImportantEvent(client: MatrixClient, timeline: Array<MatrixEvent>): MatrixEvent | null {\n    for (let index = timeline.length - 1; index >= 0; index--) {\n        const event = timeline[index];\n        if (isImportantEvent(client, event)) {\n            return event;\n        }\n    }\n    return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Given this event does not have a receipt, is it important enough to make\n * this room unread?\n */\nfunction isImportantEvent(client: MatrixClient, event: MatrixEvent): boolean {\n    return !shouldHideEvent(event) && eventTriggersUnreadCount(client, event);\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":126,"oldLines":11,"newStart":126,"newLines":15,"lines":["     return false;"," }"," ","-function doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room: Room, timeline: Array<MatrixEvent>, threadId?: string): boolean {","+function doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(","+    room: Room,","+    myUserId: string,","+    timeline: Array<MatrixEvent>,","+    threadId?: string,","+): boolean {","     // The room is a space, let's ignore it","     if (room.isSpaceRoom()) return false;"," ","-    const myUserId = room.client.getSafeUserId();","     const latestImportantEventId = findLatestImportantEvent(room.client, timeline)?.getId();","     if (latestImportantEventId) {","         return !hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, latestImportantEventId, threadId);"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

             }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    return false;\n}\n\nexport function doesRoomHaveUnreadMessages(room: Room, includeThreads: boolean): boolean {\n    // Cache userId to avoid repeated lookups in the hot path\n    const myUserId = room.client.getSafeUserId();\n    const toCheck: Array<[Room | Thread, string | undefined]> = [[room, undefined]];\n    if (includeThreads) {\n (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, myUserId, withTimeline.timeline, threadId)) {\n            // We found an unread, so the room is unread\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we got here then no timelines were found with unread messages.\n    return false;\n}\n\nfunction doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(\n    room: Room,\n    myUserId: string,\n    timeline: Array<MatrixEvent>,\n    threadId?: string,\n): boolean {\n    // The room is a space, let's ignore it\n    if (room.isSpaceRoom()) return false;\n\n    const latestImportantEventId = findLatestImportantEvent(room.client, timeline)?.getId();\n    if (latestImportantEventId) {\n        return !hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, latestImportantEventId, threadId);\n    } else {\n        // We couldn't find an important event to check - check the unimportant ones.\n        const earliestUnimportantEventId = timeline.at(0)?.getId();\n        if (!earliestUnimportantEventId) {\n            // There are no events in this timeline - it is uninitialised, so we\n            // consider it read\n            return false;\n        } else if (hasUserReadEvent(room, myUserId, earliestUnimportantEventId, threadId)) {\n            // Some of the unimportant events are read, and there are no\n            // important ones after them, so we've read everything.\n            return false;\n        } else {\n            // We have events. and none of them are read.  We must guess that\n            // the timeline is unread, because there could be older unread\n            // important events that we don't have loaded.\n            logger.warn(\"Falling back to unread room because of no read receipt or counting message found\", {\n                roomId: room.roomId,\n                earliestUnimportantEventId: earliestUnimportantEventId,\n            });\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/**\n * Returns true if this room has unread threads.\n * @param room The room to check\n * @returns {boolean} True if the given room has unread threads\n */\nexport function doesRoomHaveUnreadThreads(room: Room): boolean {\n    if (getRoomNotifsState(room.client, room.roomId) === RoomNotifState.Mute) {\n        // No unread for muted rooms, nor their threads\n        // NB. This logic duplicated in RoomNotifs.determineUnreadState\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    for (const thread of room.getThreads()) {\n        if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, thread.timeline, thread.id)) {\n            // We found an unread, so the room has an unread thread\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we got here then no threads were found with unread messages.\n    return false;\n}\n\nexport function doesRoomOrThreadHaveUnreadMessages(roomOrThread: Room | Thread): boolean {\n    const room = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.room : roomOrThread;\n    const events = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.timeline : room.getLiveTimeline().getEvents();\n    const threadId = roomOrThread instanceof Thread ? roomOrThread.id : undefined;\n    return doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, events, threadId);\n}\n\n/**\n * Look backwards through the timeline and find the last event that is\n * \"important\" in the sense of isImportantEvent.\n *\n * @returns the latest important event, or null if none were found\n */\nfunction findLatestImportantEvent(client: MatrixClient, timeline: Array<MatrixEvent>): MatrixEvent | null {\n    for (let index = timeline.length - 1; index >= 0; index--) {\n        const event = timeline[index];\n        if (isImportantEvent(client, event)) {\n            return event;\n        }\n    }\n    return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Given this event does not have a receipt, is it important enough to make\n * this room unread?\n */\nfunction isImportantEvent(client: MatrixClient, event: MatrixEvent): boolean {\n    return !shouldHideEvent(event) && eventTriggersUnreadCount(client, event);\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":174,"oldLines":8,"newStart":174,"newLines":11,"lines":["         return false;","     }"," ","+    // Cache userId to avoid repeated lookups in the hot path","+    const myUserId = room.client.getSafeUserId();","+","     for (const thread of room.getThreads()) {","-        if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, thread.timeline, thread.id)) {","+        if (doesTimelineHaveUnreadMessages(room, myUserId, thread.timeline, thread.id)) {","             // We found an unread, so the room has an unread thread","             return true;","         }"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
